title: Asuka in relation to Gendos plan
display_title: Asuka in relation to Gendos plan
wikitext:
[]: Asuka in relation to Gendo's plan             Quote Post by Güf Trüp » Sun Jul 21, 2013 5:13 pm So 3.0 has cemented that Gendo's playing a whole new ball game this time around, with the bulk of all of his plans relying on manipulating others into doing exactly what he wants them to do. Over the course of 14 years he gets Rei, Kaworu, and especially Shinji to serve as his puppets, with one of his long standing goals revolving around ascending Shinji into an angel-like form so that he can pull off his ideal impact. Rei and Kaworu serve as little more than motivators for Shinji to trigger certain key events. My question is: does Asuka have any role in his plan? Could the Bardiel incident have been predicted and specifically set up to make sure Asuka became an Angel-thing as well? This would also explain why Toji wasn't picked as a pilot this time around: all of the pilots are specifically chosen for their roles in advancing the plan. Mari seems to be an "unknown factor" brought in by an outside force that SEEMS to be an exception to this rule. What I'm asking is this: Did/does Gendo have plans for Asuka that require her to become the same kind of thing Shinji is? Is she his fail safe if Shinji screws up in the end game? Or is she meant to play a similar role to Rei and Kaworu: a way to get Shinji to do what he wants him to do through sacrificing someone close to him? I feel like Toji's absence as a pilot confirms that it's no longer a case of having a whole class full of backups to pilot the all important Evas, and that everyone was chosen specifically to play their part in his scheme.

[]: Re: Asuka in relation to Gendo's plan             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Jul 21, 2013 5:35 pm Güf Trüp wrote: My question is: does Asuka have any role in his plan? Could the Bardiel incident have been predicted and specifically set up to make sure Asuka became an Angel-thing as well? This would also explain why Toji wasn't picked as a pilot this time around: all of the pilots are specifically chosen for their roles in advancing the plan.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 21, 2013 7:49 pm I really doubt that Gendo planned what happened to Bardiel, if anyone could have planned it, it was SEELE : there was even an unused storyboard where SEELE had a private reunion(sans Gendo) where it's revealed that they are the one who made EVA-04 sabotaged and knew thanks to the SDSS what kind of Angel the 9th would be and how to make it infect EVA-03, all of this as a retaliation for what happened at Bethany Base(which was more or less the starting point of a cold war between SEELE and Gendo), the fact it wasn't used could mean that Anno had another idea or that he wanted to keep it mysterious and ambiguous, but still the idea got through his mind. But It's possible that Gendo and SEELE used the data collected from Asuka after the EVA-03 incident to develop new technologies. As for any part she could have in Gendo's plans, pre time skip I think she was just a professional Angel exterminator, useful since Shinji was purposely kept ignorant about the Evas and the Angels, and that Gendo planned to sooner or later trap Rei inside EVA-01 to make her and Shinji awaken it(and maybe he planned to let them trapped in there, in "safety", until Instrumentality), and Asuka would be useful to deal with the last ones. Now post time skip, I doubt that Gendo had any specific part planned for her except "part of WILLE" : she's too knowledgeable about SEELE, Gendo's true colors and all their crazy shenanigans with HIP to be manipulated into piloting for him(and would flat-out refuse if she's captured and asked to pilot for him) The only way I could think of Gendo planning something about Asuka is if he analyzed her personality and relationship with Shinji : everything they had gone through as teammates, what she had to live because of him(Bardiel, maybe her left eye)... and came to the conclusion that she would be the kind of person that would be too enraged in front of Shinji's "betrayal" of piloting against their orders to try to reason with him, and will directly go into combat mode, and maybe even go for the kill, which will put Shinji even more into a corner and drive him to the breaking point where he will pull out the spears, no matter what Kaworu will tell him(because obviously he will notice that something is not right with the spears), so he concluded that having Asuka fighting against Shinji would be a good thing for his plans. Which would showcase how much more colder and ruthless he became with the past 14 years : even though he was a dick 14 years ago, bringing his completely inexperienced son to forces him to fight against giant abominations through guilt-tripping and orchestrating his son getting closer to Rei so he would awaken EVA-01 along with her(and maybe keep them both trapped with Yui inside it), even never sank as low as in 3.0 where he manipulated his son into a fight to the death with his former friends and indirectly his ex-guardian, which were both the closest thing he ever had to a family.

[]:             Quote Post by AlphaGamma » Sun Jul 21, 2013 10:21 pm You know how it is with Gendo's plans... He has a final objective and every time something happens that brings him closer to his objective, that something becomes a part of his "plan". Anyway, i wouldn't be too surprised if the 4th turned out to be a confrontation between Mari and Gendo. Seele is now gone and Mari definitely seems to know more about Gendo (or to know Gendo better) than anyone else (the -kun line). Provided Mari has a plan of his own, Asuka could end up being for her what Shinji is for Gendo. Quite possibly thanks to the (speculated) contamination. Bottom line, I doubt Asuka will ever even do something for Gendo (willingly or not), but it wouldn't surprise me if she were the one to actually disrupt his plans.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jul 22, 2013 9:19 am For whatever it's worth (Doubt much, since it was a draft): Eva-2XX-Storyboards-vs-Final Reichu wrote: キール「エヴァ３号 は予定通り、第９使徒の依l代としての役目を終えた。」 「予定通り、使徒とエヴァの融合は可能であったな」 「そして、エヴァを触媒とし、我ら人類との融合の可能性も見いだせた」 Keel: As planned[1], Eva Unit 03 fulfilled its role as a medium[2] for the 9th Angel. ?: As planned, the fusion of Angel and Eva was possible. ?: And we've learned that, with Eva as the catalyst, even merging with us humans is possible. キール「希望は実現しつつある。失った人柱の犠牲にも報いねばならん」 「過酷すぎるな」 Keel: Our hopes are being realized. Even the cost of the lost human sacrifice will pay off. [???] ?: That's awfully harsh. キール「そのための子供達だ。人には全うすべきそれぞれの役がある。」 「群体としての人類。種全体が生き残るための、個々の役割か」 Keel: That is the children's purpose. Each has a role that must be achieved by (a/that?) person. [???] ?: The human colony organism. Individual duties, so that the entire species survives? キール「我らは、個体では生きていけない。[3] その業と原罪を超えるための、人類補完計画なのだ」 Keel: We must not live as(?) individuals. [3] The Human Instrumentality Project is for transcending that karma [4] and original sin, after all. [1] yotei doori! [2] yorishiro. This is the same thing that Eva-01 was becoming in EoE. Tangorin says, "object representative of a divine spirit; object to which a spirit is drawn or summoned; object or animal occupied by a kami". [3] There's a line here that was present in an earlier draft (published in back of main CRC book), excised from storyboard: だが個体故の苦しみから解放されることもない。Don't feel like trying to translate it now, mostly because I have no idea what 個体故 is. [4] Kanji can be read either as "gou", which my dictionary gives as "Buddhist karma, actions committed in a former life", or "waza" = "deed, act, work, performance", which doesn't seem to go along with "original sin" as well.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 22, 2013 12:48 pm ^ Well, that's stuff about Seele's plan (that didn't even make it in the final continuity), and Seele's plans seem to be a tad different from Gendo's plans.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jul 22, 2013 1:14 pm Wow, if that winds up being true despite its revelation having been discarded, it would explain a lot. Maybe it was discarded only because it reveals way too much way too soon.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Jul 22, 2013 1:32 pm Monk Ed wrote: Maybe it was discarded only because it reveals way too much way too soon.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jul 22, 2013 1:39 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: But why reveal it in the CRC if they're actually keeping it?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jul 22, 2013 1:55 pm Wow, I didn't remembered that SEELE's private reunion was that heavy on revelations : not only they orchestrated Bardiel infecting EVA-03 to see if an Evangelion can serve as a vessel for an Angel(they probably used this knowledge to seal the 12th Angel inside Mark.06), but that they also wanted to see what would happen to the pilot, see if it's possible to merge an Angel with an human using the Eva as a catalyst! And since the choice for the test pilot was between NERV's hand, that Gendo had doubts about SEELE's insistence to take EVA-03 and test it as soon as possible and Rei and Shinji were useful for his own agenda, that means that poor Asuka was screwed from the start, whatever Misato would have chosen!(Gendo could simply order that Asuka is to be sent instead of Rei or Shinji, and Misato would had to comply) The worst is that SEELE considered the human ending as an unwilling guinea pig for their twisted experience as a sacrifice, meaning that they didn't even imagined that Asuka would survive! Which coincidently proves just how badass she is! And finally this conference would have proved that what's happening with Asuka's eye on Q was indeed of angelic nature. TMBounty_Hunter wrote: But why reveal it in the CRC if they're actually keeping it?

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Tue Jul 23, 2013 5:54 am My original theory about EVA-03's sabotage was that was done to get Asuka out of the way, in both Gendo's and SEELE's scenarios. After all, Soryu played little role in Gendo's scenario in the original series. Perhaps this time around, Gendo and SEELE decide that Asuka is an unwanted variable in their scenario. Having read the other posts in this thread, I now think that Gendo and SEELE's plan for her was like hitting two birds with one stone - Asuka is gone, and she's also a human guinea pig for their Angel/EVA hybrid...and this is before you factor in whatever reaction the European Branch would have.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jul 23, 2013 7:42 am ^ I think that if in the final production what happened to EVA-03 and Asuka was still planned by someone, as depicted in the draft, it was by SEELE alone. Remember that Gendo wanted to delay as much as possible their experimentations on Angels, probably so SEELE won't be too powerful when the moment of their inevitable confrontation will come, that's why he made Bethany Base destroyed(and also to covertly steal the Key of N) SEELE discovered Gendo's treachery(Fuyutsuki's comment while in the Moon that "By now SEELE must have discovered NERV's true objectives") and spent the rest of the movie retaliating to weaken him(probably to more easily get rid of him later). - first probably by sabotaging EVA-04, depriving Gendo of a better battery technology(maybe even unlimited deployment time), Fuyutsuki tough that what happened in the US could have not been accidental, Kaji who was spying on them asked himself the same question, that only leave SEELE as a probable culprit - second, if this draft is kept in the canon, by orchestrating EVA-03's possession by Bardiel, remember that SEELE was awfully insistent that Gendo tests EVA-03 as soon as possible, going as far as refusing to give Gendo the funds to repair EVA-00. And by doing that SEELE could hit two birds with one stone : first by continuing their experimentations about Angel-Eva-Human interactions, and secondly by crippling a little more Gendo by depriving him of another Combat specialized Evangelion and of a pilot, putting him back at the same point that in the end of 1.0, Shinji demiting being only an unplanned but happy cherry on top of the cake for SEELE, putting Gendo almost back at square one. - thirdly, by sending Mark.06 with the LoC to finish off Zeruel, but only after he had obliterated NERV's remaining Evangelions and most of NERV's base, so that when Kaworu will intervene and save the day, NERV will have to let him stay, since he will be the only line of defense left against the Angels... at least that was the plan, Mari popping out of nowhere and Shinji winning against all odds and Awakening his Eva made the balance flinch a little more to Gendo's side, ending the confrontation at the end of 2.0 as a draw.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jul 23, 2013 8:34 am driftking18594 wrote: My original theory about EVA-03's sabotage was that was done to get Asuka out of the way, in both Gendo's and SEELE's scenarios. After all, Soryu played little role in Gendo's scenario in the original series. Perhaps this time around, Gendo and SEELE decide that Asuka is an unwanted variable in their scenario. Having read the other posts in this thread, I now think that Gendo and SEELE's plan for her was like hitting two birds with one stone - Asuka is gone, and she's also a human guinea pig for their Angel/EVA hybrid...and this is before you factor in whatever reaction the European Branch would have.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jul 23, 2013 9:03 am ^ That would imply that SEELE knew what Gendo had in mind with Rei(to use her in conjunction with Shinji to awake EVA-01). It's also possible that SEELE's goal was only to get rid of one pilot to weaken Gendo, whoever pilot ends inside that Eva-sized coffin.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jul 23, 2013 10:22 am ElMariachi wrote: ^ That would imply that SEELE knew what Gendo had in mind with Rei(to use her in conjunction with Shinji to awake EVA-01). It's also possible that SEELE's goal was only to get rid of one pilot to weaken Gendo, whoever pilot ends inside that Eva-sized coffin.

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Fri Jul 26, 2013 3:29 am My view on Gendo's intentions or plan for Asuka may not be anything pertinent nor advantageous during 3.0 with the exception of her actions against Mark.09 and the near destruction of Eva.02. However, as a lot of people have noticed, Shinji tends to form strong bonds with individuals who are either Angel or Angelic in which is used as a tool for Gendo's plan towards HIP. Now that Asuka is back on the horizon who is also angelic in nature I wouldn't be surprised Gendo's gambit is for Shinji and Asuka to bond as well for the sake of FINAL impact which is mentioned in FINAL preview.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jul 26, 2013 6:55 pm I don't think SEELE or Gendo, in neither continuity, ever planned for Asuka or Unit Two to be anything beyond a simple angel-thrashing goon, at very most something to (de)motivate Shinji with, with no further role in the big plan. Being screwed up in the process (vias Angel contamination) is just part of that. The good thing is that instead of deluding herself into thinking she is uber special and chosen, Q!Asuka is instead actively out to get SEELE and Gendo's heads, refusing to be their tool, with is kinda really badass the more you think about it. It's just sad that Asuka had to be completely broken, bitter and angry to reach that point... Gendo, of course, thinks WILLEs actions are all keikaku doori. That includes Asuka. The finished version of the bardiel stuff kinda has Gendo suggesting not to use an untested unit (which infact blows up) which makes him look like the reasonable one in the end... SEELE and Gendo are, of course, in a state of cold war for most of 2.0.
